title: [CI:DOCS] html docs: fix broken links
body:
sphinx generates broken links pointing to foo.md. Change all
of those to foo.html.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: We can try this but I am not sure if readthedocs will use our Makefile.
Ah - thanks. Where is the doc procedure documented?

Local testing is documented here https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/docs#local-testing.
As far as readthedocs is concerned you just point it at a git repo and it will build and publish it for you. I have never used readthedocs but @baude and @TomSweeneyRedHat should know more.
Well, plan B, I guess we could try custom javascript that rewrites links on page load

I think I have a better plan. https://github.com/readthedocs/recommonmark/issues/221
We should switch the markdown converter, assuming the new one can do this correctly.
Let's close this in favor of https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11614
